title: IACUC — Animal Housing Capacity
content:
The Morgan State University Animal Research Facility (ARF), located in the Martin D. Jenkins Behavioral and Social Sciences Center, publishes its current housing capacity by species and rack configuration. The facility supports laboratory rats, mice, and small aquatic species (fish) in individually ventilated cages (IVC) and aquaria distributed across multiple dedicated animal-housing rooms.

Housing capacity (full breakdown):
- Rat — individually ventilated housing in 2 rooms: 2 animals per cage, 30 cages per rack, 12 racks, total 720 animals.
- Mouse — individually ventilated housing in 2 rooms: 4 animals per cage, 80 cages per rack, 3 racks, total 960 animals.
- Mouse — individual biometric housing with 24-hour activity monitoring in 1 room: 1 animal per cage, 60 cages per rack, 1 rack, total 60 animals.
- Aquaria — housing fish in 1 room: 4 animals per tank, 12 tanks per rack, 2 racks, total 96 animals.
- TOTAL FACILITY CAPACITY: 1,836 animals.

All housing is housed within the AAALAC-accredited 2,725-square-foot vivarium built in 2017. The 60-cage biometric monitoring rack enables 24-hour continuous activity monitoring for individual mice, supporting behavioral and circadian studies. All work performed in the facility must be approved by the MSU IACUC and adheres to occupational health and safety guidelines. Contact Dr. Ingrid Tulloch (Director, Ingrid.Tulloch@morgan.edu), Rysheem McGirt (Manager, Rysheem.Mcgirt@morgan.edu), or Dr. Matthew Terzi (Clinical Veterinarian, M.Terzi@morgan.edu) for housing-allocation questions.
